title: Senior Software Engineer, Applied AI Services
skills: Scalable backend services (APIs, events, data stores, cloud infrastructure), and
Applied AI/ML systems (offline data ingestion, feature and signal pipelines, LLM/ML‑powered capabilities, evaluation frameworks, and AI‑driven workflows).
Lead end‑to‑end delivery of features — from early prototypes to production‑hardened systems.
Work deeply with AI/ML workflows while applying solid engineering and reliability practices.
Design, build, and maintain data pipelines (e.g., Spark, Databricks, Python, Kafka or equivalents) that turn raw events and signals into durable features and model inputs.

Develop and integrate ML/LLM capabilities (e.g., embeddings, similarity search, ranking, clustering, text generation) into backend flows and expose them via stable, well‑versioned services and APIs for product teams to consume.

Design and iterate on prompts, configurations, and AI workflows using notebooks and offline experimentation, and build automated evaluation systems (e.g., LLM‑as‑judge, regression suites, sampling pipelines) with clear quality metrics for accuracy, safety, latency, and cost.

Translate prototypes into reliable, observable production systems with solid tests, deployment pipelines, and on‑call readiness; own their operational health through runbooks, incident reviews, and continuous improvements, while optimizing for performance, reliability, and cost.

Collaborate closely with AI/ML and Agentic AI teams to bring 0→1 AI capabilities to customers and evolve them to 1→N, partner with SJS and HDP engineers on clean interfaces between offline pipelines, model outputs, and online APIs/events, and contribute reusable libraries, templates, and examples that help other engineers ship AI‑powered features faster.

You are strongly proficient in at least one backend programming language such as Python, Java, Kotlin, or similar, and have delivered scalable services or APIs to production in a cloud environment (e.g., AWS, GCP), including thoughtful data modeling and performance tuning.
You have hands‑on experience with data processing or distributed systems (e.g., Spark, Databricks, Kafka or similar data pipelines) and are comfortable designing and consuming APIs (REST or GraphQL).
You’ve worked with ML or LLM‑based systems and shipped at least one feature or capability to production, in partnership with an AI/ML team or self‑built.
You’re familiar with embeddings, ranking, clustering, recommendations, or other ML applications that support personalization and search/browse experiences.
You have experience with prompt engineering and LLM‑based product development, including safety/guardrail considerations and offline/online evaluation frameworks for AI‑powered features.

You are comfortable in modern infrastructure environments (e.g., Kubernetes‑based deployments, event‑driven architectures, and observability stacks) and can translate prototypes into robust, observable, well‑tested production systems.

You have a track record of owning projects end‑to‑end — from requirements and design through implementation, rollout, operations, and cross‑functional collaboration with product, design, and data science partners.
Here at Zillow, we value the experience and perspective of candidates with non‑traditional backgrounds. We encourage you to apply if you have transferable skills or related experiences.
description:
About the job

About The Team

Shopper Journey Services (SJS) is Zillow’s engine for authenticated user intent and a core enabler of Home Details Page (HDP) experiences. We gather rich, explicit and implicit signals from authenticated shoppers and co‑shoppers, refine them into durable models, services, and display‑ready data, and activate those capabilities across Zillow’s ecosystem to deliver personalized, high‑quality experiences. Our charter spans two tightly connected areas: User Intent & Applied AI, where we build the pipelines, services, and evaluation capabilities that turn user signals into intelligence and AI‑powered experiences, and the HDP Backend Platform, where we power HDP with robust, low‑latency, display‑ready data. AI is both a product capability and an engineering accelerator for this team, and we are investing in LLM‑ and ML‑powered workflows, evaluation, and agentic systems while modernizing our conventional backend stack.

About The Role

We are looking for a Senior Software Engineer, Applied AI Services to build and scale end‑to‑end intelligent systems that power personalized experiences and HDP‑aligned capabilities.

This is a hands‑on IC role where you’ll spend most of your time writing code and driving technical design for impactful production systems. You will operate at the intersection of:


Scalable backend services (APIs, events, data stores, cloud infrastructure), and
Applied AI/ML systems (offline data ingestion, feature and signal pipelines, LLM/ML‑powered capabilities, evaluation frameworks, and AI‑driven workflows). 


You will help turn user intent into intelligence, safely bring new AI capabilities from 0→1, and scale them across Zillow’s shopper journey surfaces.

You Will Get To


Lead end‑to‑end delivery of features — from early prototypes to production‑hardened systems. 
Work deeply with AI/ML workflows while applying solid engineering and reliability practices. 
Design, build, and maintain data pipelines (e.g., Spark, Databricks, Python, Kafka or equivalents) that turn raw events and signals into durable features and model inputs. 
Develop and integrate ML/LLM capabilities (e.g., embeddings, similarity search, ranking, clustering, text generation) into backend flows and expose them via stable, well‑versioned services and APIs for product teams to consume. 
Design and iterate on prompts, configurations, and AI workflows using notebooks and offline experimentation, and build automated evaluation systems (e.g., LLM‑as‑judge, regression suites, sampling pipelines) with clear quality metrics for accuracy, safety, latency, and cost. 
Translate prototypes into reliable, observable production systems with solid tests, deployment pipelines, and on‑call readiness; own their operational health through runbooks, incident reviews, and continuous improvements, while optimizing for performance, reliability, and cost. 
Collaborate closely with AI/ML and Agentic AI teams to bring 0→1 AI capabilities to customers and evolve them to 1→N, partner with SJS and HDP engineers on clean interfaces between offline pipelines, model outputs, and online APIs/events, and contribute reusable libraries, templates, and examples that help other engineers ship AI‑powered features faster. 


This role has been categorized as a teleworker position. Teleworkers do not have a permanent corporate office workplace and instead work from a physical location of their choice, which must be identified to the Company. Employees may live anywhere in Mexico, with availability to travel to Mexico City, as we recommend attendance at occasional office events.

In addition to a competitive base salary and benefits, this position is also eligible for equity awards based on factors such as experience, performance and location.

Who you are


You are strongly proficient in at least one backend programming language such as Python, Java, Kotlin, or similar, and have delivered scalable services or APIs to production in a cloud environment (e.g., AWS, GCP), including thoughtful data modeling and performance tuning. 
You have hands‑on experience with data processing or distributed systems (e.g., Spark, Databricks, Kafka or similar data pipelines) and are comfortable designing and consuming APIs (REST or GraphQL). 
You’ve worked with ML or LLM‑based systems and shipped at least one feature or capability to production, in partnership with an AI/ML team or self‑built. 
You’re familiar with embeddings, ranking, clustering, recommendations, or other ML applications that support personalization and search/browse experiences. 
You have experience with prompt engineering and LLM‑based product development, including safety/guardrail considerations and offline/online evaluation frameworks for AI‑powered features. 
You are comfortable in modern infrastructure environments (e.g., Kubernetes‑based deployments, event‑driven architectures, and observability stacks) and can translate prototypes into robust, observable, well‑tested production systems. 
You have a track record of owning projects end‑to‑end — from requirements and design through implementation, rollout, operations, and cross‑functional collaboration with product, design, and data science partners. 
Here at Zillow, we value the experience and perspective of candidates with non‑traditional backgrounds. We encourage you to apply if you have transferable skills or related experiences. 


Get to know us

At Zillow, we’re reimagining how people move—through the real estate market and through their careers. As the most-visited real estate platform in the U.S., we help customers navigate buying, selling, financing and renting with greater ease and confidence. Whether you're working in tech, sales, operations, or design, you’ll be part of a company that's reshaping an industry and helping more people make home a reality.

Zillow is honored to be recognized among the best workplaces in the country. Zillow was named one of FORTUNE 100 Best Companies to Work For® in 2025, and included on the PEOPLE Companies That Care® 2025 list, reflecting our commitment to creating an innovative, inclusive, and engaging culture where employees are empowered to grow.

No matter where you sit in the organization, your work will help drive innovation, support our customers, and move the industry—and your career—forward, together.

Zillow Group is an equal opportunity employer committed to fostering an inclusive, innovative environment with the best employees. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please contact your recruiter directly.

Qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment in accordance with applicable state and local law.

Los Angeles County applicants: Job duties for this position include: work safely and cooperatively with other employees, supervisors, and staff; adhere to standards of excellence despite stressful conditions; communicate effectively and respectfully with employees, supervisors, and staff to ensure exceptional customer service; and follow all federal, state, and local laws and Company policies. Criminal history may have a direct, adverse, and negative relationship with some of the material job duties of this position. These include the duties and responsibilities listed above, as well as the abilities to adhere to company policies, exercise sound judgment, effectively manage stress and work safely and respectfully with others, exhibit trustworthiness and professionalism, and safeguard business operations and the Company’s reputation. Pursuant to the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance, we will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records.

title: Senior Web Analyst – Adobe Analytics & Journey Analytics
skills: Act as the primary Adobe Analytics expert for sandisk.com, owning Analysis Workspace structure, variable strategy, and data usability.
Design, build, and maintain scalable dashboards and workspace projects, from executive summaries to deep-dive exploratory analyses.
Establish and document a clear, consistent variable taxonomy that enables reliable self-service reporting.
Serve as a role model and enablement partner for a small group of internal Analytics power users.
Design and maintain standardized Adobe Analytics Workspace templates that serve as trusted, reusable sources of truth for common business questions.
Own the quality and evolution of both the Adobe Analytics and Amplitude Growth Analytics implementations.
Partner with IT and development teams to write detailed measurement requirements.
Influence tagging strategy within Adobe Launch.
Ensure analytics implementations reflect strong understanding of browser behavior and tagging mechanics.
Conduct advanced clickstream, pathing, and flow analysis.
Analyze end-to-end digital customer journeys.
Demonstrate how the website delivers business value beyond last-click conversion.
Partner with Web Strategy, UX, and eCommerce stakeholders to use Amplitude Growth Analytics for journey analysis, behavioral segmentation, and hypothesis-driven exploration.
Help define event, property, and user schema in Amplitude in coordination with Adobe Analytics variable strategy.
Translate business questions into Amplitude analyses such as funnels, paths, cohorts, and retention views.
Ensure consistency and alignment between Adobe Analytics and Amplitude data definitions, metrics, and narratives.
Contribute to documentation and enablement so teams understand when to use Adobe Analytics vs. Amplitude.
Use Amplitude to answer journey, engagement, and behavioral questions, not to duplicate standard Adobe Analytics reporting.

description:
About the job

Company Description

Sandisk understands how people and businesses consume data and we relentlessly innovate to deliver solutions that enable today’s needs and tomorrow’s next big ideas. With a rich history of groundbreaking innovations in Flash and advanced memory technologies, our solutions have become the beating heart of the digital world we’re living in and that we have the power to shape.

Sandisk meets people and businesses at the intersection of their aspirations and the moment, enabling them to keep moving and pushing possibility forward. We do this through the balance of our powerhouse manufacturing capabilities and our industry-leading portfolio of products that are recognized globally for innovation, performance and quality.

Sandisk has two facilities recognized by the World Economic Forum as part of the Global Lighthouse Network for advanced 4IR innovations. These facilities were also recognized as Sustainability Lighthouses for breakthroughs in efficient operations. With our global reach, we ensure the global supply chain has access to the Flash memory it needs to keep our world moving forward.

Job Description

Sandisk is seeking a Senior Web Analyst to serve as the primary expert for Adobe Analytics across sandisk.com. This role is responsible for ensuring our digital measurement is intentional, well-documented, and genuinely useful.

In addition to AA, Sandisk is expanding its analytics capabilities with Amplitude Growth Analytics to better understand user journeys, engagement patterns, and behavioral drivers across sandisk.com. This role will help define how Adobe Analytics and Amplitude work together, ensuring each tool is used intentionally for the questions it answers best.

You will be the connective tissue between data, technology, and decision-making. While the web strategy team has content strategists, UX designers, and dedicated SEO and technical audit partners, this role provides the deep analytics expertise those teams rely on to understand engagement,performance, customer journeys, and business impact. You will shape how data is collected, structured, interpreted, and self-served across the web, e-commerce, and performance marketing organization.

This is an individual contributor role for someone who takes pride in high-quality instrumentation, loves clickstream analysis, and understands how thoughtful measurement unlocks better experiences and better business outcomes. This is an in‑house role focused on long‑term analytics quality and business enablement rather than client delivery.

Essential Duties And Responsibilities

Adobe Analytics Ownership & Enablement


Act as the primary Adobe Analytics expert for sandisk.com, owning Analysis Workspace structure, variable strategy, and data usability.
Design, build, and maintain scalable dashboards and workspace projects, from executive summaries to deep-dive exploratory analyses.
Establish and document a clear, consistent variable taxonomy that enables reliable self-service reporting.
Serve as a role model and enablement partner for a small group of internal Analytics power users.
Design and maintain standardized Adobe Analytics Workspace templates that serve as trusted, reusable sources of truth for common business questions.


Measurement Strategy & Implementation


Own the quality and evolution of both the Adobe Analytics and Amplitude Growth Analytics implementations.
Partner with IT and development teams to write detailed measurement requirements.
Influence tagging strategy within Adobe Launch.
Ensure analytics implementations reflect strong understanding of browser behavior and tagging mechanics.


Clickstream & Customer Journey Analysis


Conduct advanced clickstream, pathing, and flow analysis.
Analyze end-to-end digital customer journeys.
Demonstrate how the website delivers business value beyond last-click conversion.


Journey & Behavioral Analytics (Amplitude)


Partner with Web Strategy, UX, and eCommerce stakeholders to use Amplitude Growth Analytics for journey analysis, behavioral segmentation, and hypothesis-driven exploration.
Help define event, property, and user schema in Amplitude in coordination with Adobe Analytics variable strategy.
Translate business questions into Amplitude analyses such as funnels, paths, cohorts, and retention views.
Ensure consistency and alignment between Adobe Analytics and Amplitude data definitions, metrics, and narratives.
Contribute to documentation and enablement so teams understand when to use Adobe Analytics vs. Amplitude.
Use Amplitude to answer journey, engagement, and behavioral questions, not to duplicate standard Adobe Analytics reporting.


Communication & Influence


Act as the senior analytics subject‑matter expert for sandisk.com, setting standards and guiding analytical decision‑making.
Serve as a thought partner on analytics platform strategy, helping Sandisk evolve from page-level reporting toward journey and behavior-led decision-making.
Communicate insights and recommendations clearly in English to US‑based and global stakeholders.
Partner with Web Strategy, SEO, UX, Content, and Product teams.
Advocate for meaningful, high-quality measurement.


Success in This Role Looks Like


A cleaner, more intentional Adobe Analytics implementation.
High confidence in reported data across teams.
Actionable journey insights that influence UX, content, or conversion optimization decisions.
Self-service reporting enabled through Workspace templates.
Clear demonstration of website business value.
Trusted partnership with Web Strategy, UX, and Engineering teams in a cross‑functional, multinational environment.


Qualifications

REQUIRED:


8+ years of experience in digital or web analytics roles.
Deep expertise with Adobe Analytics and Analysis Workspace.
Strong working knowledge of Adobe Launch or similar tag management system.
Experience writing analytics requirements for IT teams.
Understanding of browser-based tracking and tag management.
Experience with clickstream and behavioral data analysis.
Familiarity with Google Search Console.
Excellent communication skills.
Professional fluency in spoken and writtenEnglish, with experience presenting insights to US‑based stakeholders.


Preferred


Experience with enterprise or global ecommerce websites.
Experience working with multiple analytics platforms in defined roles.
Experience with product or journey analytics tools such as Amplitude.
Experience enabling analytics self‑service.
Strong documentation discipline.


Additional Information

Sandisk thrives on the power and potential of diversity. As a global company, we believe the most effective way to embrace the diversity of our customers and communities is to mirror it from within. We believe the fusion of various perspectives results in the best outcomes for our employees, our company, our customers, and the world around us. We are committed to an inclusive environment where every individual can thrive through a sense of belonging, respect and contribution.

Sandisk is committed to offering opportunities to applicants with disabilities and ensuring all candidates can successfully navigate our careers website and our hiring process. Please contact us at jobs.accommodations@sandisk.comto advise us of your accommodation request. In your email, please include a description of the specific accommodation you are requesting as well as the job title and requisition number of the position for which you are applying.

description:
About the job

Netflix is one of the world's leading entertainment services, with over 300 million paid memberships in over 190 countries enjoying TV series, films and games across a wide variety of genres and languages. Members can play, pause and resume watching as much as they want, anytime, anywhere, and can change their plans at any time.

About The Role

Netflix derives a significant competitive advantage from the analytical approach it takes to manage all aspects of the business. As we continue to push for growth and retention in the LATAM region, we are looking for a Manager to join our Partnerships F&S team. This role will be based in Mexico City and it includes limited travel (a few weeks per year) to partner locations in LATAM and our headquarters in Los Gatos, California.

Partnerships are a critical driver of growth for Netflix. The most common types of partnerships with local operators are bundles. These impact millions of members, and our analyses play a critical role in the assessment and ongoing strategy for those partnerships in the region. During negotiations, we provide guidance to the Business Development team using data and analysis. When partnerships are launched, we monitor their performance and search for best practices and areas of improvement. Finally, we work closely with our Product and Partner Engagement teams to determine which changes in the Netflix experience could improve the partnerships’ impact.

Responsibilities


Evaluating the financial impact of partnership opportunities (primarily bundles)
Financial projections and scenario modeling 
Risk identification and mitigation strategies 
Advising the Business Development team during the negotiations 
Analyzing the performance of partnerships to extract actionable insights (e.g. best practices, areas of improvement) 
Conducting global analyses regarding bundle performance 
Creating insightful presentations that facilitate decision-making for internal stakeholders and external partners 
Partnering with the Product team to help identify/prioritize and assess the impact of initiatives (e.g. improving Netflix plan upgrades for bundles) 
Providing inputs to other teams working on partnerships initiatives (E.g. Data Science & Engineering, Consumer Insights) 
Developing industry expertise for the US and Canada

Qualifications

Candidates are required to be highly analytical and effective communicators. Prior experience in management consulting or an equivalent analytical role is strongly preferred. Ideal candidates will have a proven track record of performing comprehensive analyses independently and proactively. They must thrive in a fast-paced environment, possess a high level of intellectual curiosity, focus on generating results and exhibit the highest standards of integrity and ethics.

Requirements


7+ years of experience, ideally in management consulting or a similar field
Extensive experience conducting quantitative analysis that leads to actionable insights
Team player with strong interpersonal skills, but also able to work independently 
Adept at managing multiple projects and stakeholders 
Skilled in preparing effective presentations with a clear narrative 
Excellent verbal and written communication skills
Strong Excel skills (mandatory) 
Proficiency with Tableau and SQL a strong plus
Fluency in Spanish a strong plus
Bachelor’s or master’s degree with emphasis on coursework of a quantitative nature 


Netflix is a unique culture and environment. Learn more here.

Inclusion is a Netflix value and we strive to host a meaningful interview experience for all candidates. If you want an accommodation/adjustment for a disability or any other reason during the hiring process, please send a request to your recruiting partner.

We are an equal-opportunity employer and celebrate diversity, recognizing that diversity builds stronger teams. We approach diversity and inclusion seriously and thoughtfully. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, ancestry, national origin, caste, sex, sexual orientation, gender, gender identity or expression, age, disability, medical condition, pregnancy, genetic makeup, marital status, or military service.

description:
About the job

Job Title: JavaScript Developer (Remote)
Work Mode: Fully Remote
Schedule: Flexible (up to 40 hours per week, based on project availability)




Role Overview:

We are looking for software engineers across Latin America to join a paid remote AI training program. In this role, you will review and evaluate AI-generated code, rate AI responses for accuracy and quality, and write coding challenges to help improve AI model performance. This is a flexible, remote opportunity well-suited for software engineers seeking project-based, asynchronous work. No prior AI experience required.




Key Responsibilities

Review and evaluate code written by AI models for accuracy, efficiency, and best practices
Rate AI responses across key dimensions including correctness, instruction-following, and quality
Write original computer science questions and coding challenges to test AI capabilities
Identify bugs, errors, and inconsistencies in AI-generated code using structured guidelines
Provide clear, constructive, and structured written feedback to improve AI responses
Follow project guidelines, quality standards, and confidentiality requirements




Qualifications

Software engineering experience — junior, mid-level, or senior welcome
Proficiency in one or more of the following: Python, Java, JavaScript, TypeScript, C++, Swift, or Rust
Strong attention to detail, analytical thinking, and problem-solving skills
Background in full stack, backend, frontend, mobile, or any engineering discipline




Eligibility Requirements

Candidates must be legally authorised to work in one of the following locations:
Argentina, Bolivia, Brazil, Belize, Chile, Colombia, Costa Rica, Dominican Republic, Dominica, Ecuador, El Salvador, Guatemala, Honduras, Mexico, Nicaragua, Panama, Paraguay, Peru, Puerto Rico, Trinidad and Tobago, Uruguay, Venezuela




Compensation

Earn up to $34 USD/hr — core project rate with additional performance incentives averaging 7.5% on top
Payments issued weekly via supported payout platforms (e.g., PayPal or AirTM)




Work Arrangement

Fully remote and asynchronous
Flexible workload depending on project availability
Free access to leading AI tools worth $200+/month included




Equal Opportunity Statement Selection decisions are based solely on skills, qualifications, and project requirements. We are committed to inclusive and fair engagement practices and consider all qualified applicants without regard to legally protected characteristics.




Ready to put your coding skills to work on AI? Apply now — interviews begin within days of application.

description:
About the job

SEEDZ GENERATION 2026

Build What's Next. Own Your Growth. 🌱

En L'Oréal creemos que los futuros líderes no se definen únicamente por los años de experiencia, sino por su capacidad para aprender, adaptarse y generar impacto.

SeedZ es la experiencia de desarrollo acelerado de L'Oréal, diseñada para identificar y preparar a la próxima generación de líderes de la compañía.

¿Qué es SeedZ?

No es un programa tradicional. Es una experiencia diseñada para acelerar tu potencial.

Durante 18 meses vivirás un viaje de aprendizaje único, donde conocerás distintas áreas del negocio, trabajarás en proyectos reales, colaborarás con líderes de la organización y desarrollarás las capacidades que impulsarán tu crecimiento profesional.

Buscamos profesionales con ganas de crecer, aprender y desafiarse constantemente:


🎓 Ser egresado(a).

Nuestro proceso de selección

description:
About the job

¿Quiénes somos?

En VEMO, somos una empresa de electromovilidad impulsada por su gente y tecnología, que integra todo el ecosistema de movilidad limpia para México y América Latina. Nuestra misión es acelerar la adopción de soluciones de movilidad limpia, mejorando la calidad de vida urbana, preservando el medio ambiente y generando empleo sostenible de alto impacto. Estamos comprometidos con una movilidad responsable, limpia e inteligente.




Propósito del rol

El VCN Data Analyst será responsable de centralizar, analizar y traducir los datos comerciales, operativos y de portafolio de la red de recarga VCN en inteligencia accionable que impulse la toma de decisiones estratégicas, la eficiencia operativa y la escalabilidad de capacidades internas. Este rol trabaja de forma transversal con líderes de área y en estrecha colaboración con los equipos de Business Intelligence, Business Development y VCN Operations para diseñar dashboards, reportes y herramientas que fortalezcan el seguimiento de indicadores clave de la red.




Requisitos

Licenciatura en Ingeniería, Actuaría, Economía, Matemáticas Aplicadas, Sistemas Computacionales o afines
Deseable: Maestría o posgrado en Data Science, Business Analytics o Business Intelligence
5+ años en análisis de datos, business analytics o roles similares, idealmente en industrias de tecnología, movilidad, energía o infraestructura
Experiencia en forecasting, modelos de predicción y optimización de operaciones
Historial comprobable en diseño de dashboards y automatización de reportes
Experiencia trabajando con stakeholders de alto nivel y generando recomendaciones estratégicas




Habilidades técnicas (Hard Skills)

Dominio avanzado de Excel y PowerPoint
Manejo de herramientas de visualización: Power BI, Tableau
Conocimientos en SQL, Python o R para análisis y modelado de datos
Sólida comprensión de métricas financieras y operativas
Experiencia en data cleaning, estructuración de bases de datos y metodologías de análisis




Habilidades interpersonales (Soft Skills)

Capacidad analítica y pensamiento estratégico
Estructura y atención al detalle
Autonomía y ownership mentality
Comunicación efectiva y data storytelling
Adaptabilidad y colaboración transversal




Nice to Have

Conocimientos en modelos de pricing y customer segmentation
Experiencia previa en empresas de alto crecimiento o scale-ups




Lo que ofrecemos

Ser parte de una empresa pionera en el ecosistema de movilidad limpia en Latinoamérica
Exposición directa a equipos directivos y senior stakeholders
Oportunidad de impactar en decisiones estratégicas y en el crecimiento de la red de recarga más importante del país
Entorno dinámico, innovador y colaborativo

description:
About the job

Our Purpose

Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential.

Title And Summary

Associate Managing Consultant, Advisors & Consulting Services, Marketing

ADVISORS & CONSULTING SERVICES

Services within Mastercard is responsible for acquiring, engaging, and retaining customers by managing fraud and risk, enhancing cybersecurity, and improving the digital payments experience. We provide value-added services and leverage expertise, data-driven insights, and execution. Our Advisors & Consulting Services team combines traditional management consulting with Mastercard’s rich data assets, proprietary platforms, and technologies to provide clients with powerful strategic insights and recommendations. Our teams work with a diverse global customer base across industries, from banking and payments to retail and restaurants.

The Advisors & Consulting Services group has five specializations: Strategy & Transformation, Performance Analytics, Business Experimentation, Marketing, and Program Management. Our Marketing consultants lead the strategy development and implementation of campaigns and engagements for clients. They use customer data to provide deep direct marketing expertise across channels, evaluating campaign results and adapting the approach to optimize marketing performance.

Positions for different specializations and levels are available in separate job postings. Please review our consulting specializations to learn more about all opportunities and apply for the position that is best suited to your background and experience: https://careers.mastercard.com/us/en/consulting-specializations-at-mastercard

Envision this role as the pivot in a thrilling marketing services transformation. It's about fully embracing and fueling change while seamlessly balancing traditional marketing endeavors. This profile thrives in a dynamic environment, contributing ideas, and actively steering this transformational journey.

Roles And Responsibilities

Client Impact


 Deliver exceptional digital marketing initiatives guided by data analytics, ensuring measurable business impact
 Take charge of client projects, collaborating to conceptualize and execute impactful campaigns
 Lead the creation and end-to-end execution of Marketing Services projects focusing on financial performance and impact
 Implement data-driven digital marketing solutions, including campaigns, marketing automation, and lifecycle management initiatives
 Ensure operational project execution within agreed milestones and budget
 Manage creative development and media planning, meeting high-quality standards aligned with client needs
 Assess, recommend and improve the performance of client’s marketing activities by using data insights and data visualization tools



Team Collaboration & Culture


 Collaborate with internal and external marketing partners to support business growth



Qualifications


 Hands-on Experience: extended experience in integrated marketing, with a strong focus on digital marketing planning and data-driven implementation
 Client Management: Proven track record in managing client accounts and leading digital marketing solutions
 Project Execution: Ability to plan and execute marketing strategies and campaigns across key marketing channels to drive impactful outcomes
 Collaboration: Thrive in a matrix organization structure, manage internal and external stakeholders effectively
 Communication: Excellent communication and presentation skills, capable of influencing colleagues and clients on the value of digital marketing projects
 Innovation: Show the ability to ideate, manage, and roll out complex and competitive digital marketing initiatives
 Education: Relevant marketing degree or equivalent experience needed
 Ability to communicate effectively in English and the local office language (if applicable)
 Eligibility to work in the country where you are applying, as well as apply for travel visas as required by travel needs



OUR PURPOSE

We work to connect and power an inclusive, digital economy that benefits everyone, everywhere by making transactions safe, simple, smart, and accessible. Using secure data and networks, partnerships and passion, our innovations and solutions help individuals, financial institutions, governments, and businesses realize their greatest potential. Our decency quotient, or DQ, drives our culture and everything we do inside and outside of our company. We cultivate a culture of inclusion for all employees that respect their individual strengths, views, and experiences. We believe that our differences enable us to be a better team – one that makes better decisions, drives innovation, and delivers better business results.

Corporate Security Responsibility

 All activities involving access to Mastercard assets, information, and networks comes with an inherent risk to the organization and, therefore, it is expected that every person working for, or on behalf of, Mastercard is responsible for information security and must:


Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices;
Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed;
Report any suspected information security violation or breach, and
Complete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines.

description:
About the job

Job Title: Frontend Developer (Remote)
Work Mode: Fully Remote
Schedule: Flexible (up to 40 hours per week, based on project availability)




Role Overview:

We are looking for software engineers across Latin America to join a paid remote AI training program. In this role, you will review and evaluate AI-generated code, rate AI responses for accuracy and quality, and write coding challenges to help improve AI model performance. This is a flexible, remote opportunity well-suited for software engineers seeking project-based, asynchronous work. No prior AI experience required.




Key Responsibilities

Review and evaluate code written by AI models for accuracy, efficiency, and best practices
Rate AI responses across key dimensions including correctness, instruction-following, and quality
Write original computer science questions and coding challenges to test AI capabilities
Identify bugs, errors, and inconsistencies in AI-generated code using structured guidelines
Provide clear, constructive, and structured written feedback to improve AI responses
Follow project guidelines, quality standards, and confidentiality requirements




Qualifications

Software engineering experience — junior, mid-level, or senior welcome
Proficiency in one or more of the following: Python, Java, JavaScript, TypeScript, C++, Swift, or Rust
Strong attention to detail, analytical thinking, and problem-solving skills
Background in full stack, backend, frontend, mobile, or any engineering discipline




Eligibility Requirements

Candidates must be legally authorised to work in one of the following locations:
Argentina, Bolivia, Brazil, Belize, Chile, Colombia, Costa Rica, Dominican Republic, Dominica, Ecuador, El Salvador, Guatemala, Honduras, Mexico, Nicaragua, Panama, Paraguay, Peru, Puerto Rico, Trinidad and Tobago, Uruguay, Venezuela




Compensation

Earn up to $34 USD/hr — core project rate with additional performance incentives averaging 7.5% on top
Payments issued weekly via supported payout platforms (e.g., PayPal or AirTM)




Work Arrangement

Fully remote and asynchronous
Flexible workload depending on project availability
Free access to leading AI tools worth $200+/month included




Equal Opportunity Statement Selection decisions are based solely on skills, qualifications, and project requirements. We are committed to inclusive and fair engagement practices and consider all qualified applicants without regard to legally protected characteristics.




Ready to put your coding skills to work on AI? Apply now — interviews begin within days of application.

title: Screening Specialist, Mexico
skills: Screening Assets - Manage comprehensive asset mastering for all tiers of Netflix screenings, collaborating with filmmakers/showrunners and Netflix Post teams to ensure 100% technical integrity, peak presentation quality, and error-free delivery across all events.
Screening Asset Distribution - Coordinating with event leads and the venue to ensure the correct content version is delivered securely, on time, and with no playback issues.
Screening Production - Executing flawless in-person and virtual screenings, including equipment setup, calibration, filmmaker/showrunner attended tech checks, and show playback.
Creation of Screening Assets including but not limited to: DCPs, Media Files (h264 and Apple ProRes), DVDs/BDs, viewing links, and more.
Work closely with Screenings and Post teams to anticipate asset needs in advance of incoming screening requests.
Familiarizing themselves with their assigned titles to anticipate potential issues (aspect ratios, forced narratives).
Familiarizing themselves with the associated title campaigns to better anticipate and prioritize timelines, budgets and workload.
Building and tracking asset delivery timelines to ensure assets are completed in advance of relevant campaign dates and incoming screening requests.
Communicate effectively with various cross-functional teams to ensure proper planning, availability and delivery of material.
Maintain, curate and expand a regional network of digital cinema mastering and distribution facilities and technical vendors.
Liaise with filmmakers to complete necessary cuts/edits for the creation of screening assets, and coordinate proper QC of the final asset.
Conduct qualitative review of the created files to ensure they are correct and meet the creation specifications.
Manage scheduling, tracking, and verification of international deliveries of screenings, festivals, and theatrical assets to venues.
Manage digital cinema audio/visual engineers at premieres and special screenings.
Troubleshooting file based delivery and creation issues in a quick and constructive manner.

This position is part of the broader Marketing organization and sits within the Events function. The ideal candidate will be able to effectively prioritize tasks and initiatives from a marketing perspective in alignment with broader marketing objectives.
This role is based in Mexico City, but the ideal candidate should have the flexibility to travel abroad if needed.

Event production- Knowledge of the event production ecosystem in Mexico, including suppliers, venues, average market prices, and local expert contacts according to the event's needs. Leadership and operational capacity to run an event from a technical standpoint.
ROS Execution - Oversight of complete in-theatre event run of show, from Q&As to musical performances or presentations.
A/V Displays & Systems - Beyond cinema projection, delivering optimal presentation on anything from TVs to large LED walls, whether in-theatre or at the event.
Lighting Design - Provide in-theatre stage and room lighting to coincide with the ambience and feel of the overall event.
A/V Capture - Coordinate with video and photo teams for in-theatre setup and lighting for recording and live-streaming.
Core technical skills & being a proactive project manager within the context of being an “Unusually Responsible Person”.
Proactivity and motivation.
The specialist needs to be strong enough in their work to support the team in achieving greater efficiencies and operational excellence.
Effective communication that can liaise between internal stakeholders and external partners.
Prioritization and working within the setting of “Context not Control”, with strong time management skills, and being able to work on multiple projects at the same time.
Being able to make informed decisions aligned with project needs
Ability to speak/write in Spanish and English is essential.
description:
About the job

Netflix is one of the world's leading entertainment services, with over 300 million paid memberships in over 190 countries enjoying TV series, films and games across a wide variety of genres and languages. Members can play, pause and resume watching as much as they want, anytime, anywhere, and can change their plans at any time.

About The Role.

The Screenings Specialist role focuses on the end-to-end work required to deliver Netflix content to off-service audiences at the best possible quality. The core of this work comprises:


Screening Assets - Manage comprehensive asset mastering for all tiers of Netflix screenings, collaborating with filmmakers/showrunners and Netflix Post teams to ensure 100% technical integrity, peak presentation quality, and error-free delivery across all events.
Screening Asset Distribution - Coordinating with event leads and the venue to ensure the correct content version is delivered securely, on time, and with no playback issues. 
Screening Production - Executing flawless in-person and virtual screenings, including equipment setup, calibration, filmmaker/showrunner attended tech checks, and show playback.


In addition, the team provides asset-heavy support for a high volume of award submissions and VOD screening links.

The Screening Specialist needs to have a strong understanding of digital cinema packages and media creation. An expertise in post-production, theatrical screenings, and distribution/operations, with experience working on promotional events where flexibility is crucial for success. They should be able to communicate technical information to both technical and non-technical audiences.

Key Responsibilities


Creation of Screening Assets including but not limited to: DCPs, Media Files (h264 and Apple ProRes), DVDs/BDs, viewing links, and more.
Work closely with Screenings and Post teams to anticipate asset needs in advance of incoming screening requests.
Familiarizing themselves with their assigned titles to anticipate potential issues (aspect ratios, forced narratives).
Familiarizing themselves with the associated title campaigns to better anticipate and prioritize timelines, budgets and workload.
Building and tracking asset delivery timelines to ensure assets are completed in advance of relevant campaign dates and incoming screening requests.
Communicate effectively with various cross-functional teams to ensure proper planning, availability and delivery of material.
Maintain, curate and expand a regional network of digital cinema mastering and distribution facilities and technical vendors.
Liaise with filmmakers to complete necessary cuts/edits for the creation of screening assets, and coordinate proper QC of the final asset.
Conduct qualitative review of the created files to ensure they are correct and meet the creation specifications.
Manage scheduling, tracking, and verification of international deliveries of screenings, festivals, and theatrical assets to venues.
Manage digital cinema audio/visual engineers at premieres and special screenings.
Troubleshooting file based delivery and creation issues in a quick and constructive manner.
This position is part of the broader Marketing organization and sits within the Events function. The ideal candidate will be able to effectively prioritize tasks and initiatives from a marketing perspective in alignment with broader marketing objectives.
This role is based in Mexico City, but the ideal candidate should have the flexibility to travel abroad if needed.


Expertise

The ideal candidate for this role should have expertise in the following topics: 


Event production- Knowledge of the event production ecosystem in Mexico, including suppliers, venues, average market prices, and local expert contacts according to the event's needs. Leadership and operational capacity to run an event from a technical standpoint.
ROS Execution - Oversight of complete in-theatre event run of show, from Q&As to musical performances or presentations.
A/V Displays & Systems - Beyond cinema projection, delivering optimal presentation on anything from TVs to large LED walls, whether in-theatre or at the event.
Lighting Design - Provide in-theatre stage and room lighting to coincide with the ambience and feel of the overall event. 
A/V Capture - Coordinate with video and photo teams for in-theatre setup and lighting for recording and live-streaming.


Skills


Core technical skills & being a proactive project manager within the context of being an “Unusually Responsible Person”.
Proactivity and motivation. 
The specialist needs to be strong enough in their work to support the team in achieving greater efficiencies and operational excellence.
Effective communication that can liaise between internal stakeholders and external partners.
Prioritization and working within the setting of “Context not Control”, with strong time management skills, and being able to work on multiple projects at the same time. 
Being able to make informed decisions aligned with project needs
Ability to speak/write in Spanish and English is essential.

title: Marketing Associate
skills: GTM Support: Contribute to go-to-market planning and execution.

description:
About the job

Canonical is a leading provider of open source software and operating systems to the global enterprise and technology markets. Our platform, Ubuntu, is very widely used in breakthrough enterprise initiatives such as public cloud, data science, AI, engineering innovation, and IoT. Our customers include the world's leading public cloud and silicon providers, and industry leaders in many sectors. The company is a pioneer of global distributed collaboration, with 1200+ colleagues in 75+ countries and very few office-based roles. Teams meet two to four times yearly in person, in interesting locations around the world, to align on strategy and execution.

The company is founder-led, profitable, and growing.

Open Role: Marketing Associate 

Marketers at Canonical are brand stewards and demand creators.They transform complex open-source technology into messages, stories and strategies that shape the market understanding of our vision. Some focus on differentiated product and portfolio positioning; others specialize in account-based engagement, partner and alliance messaging, or product-led growth. Whatever their specialization, each marketer works as part of a cross-functional unit, contributing campaigns and insights that help the company learn and grow faster.

We have a bias for action, focus efforts on the most impactful activities and take ownership of our results. We work across long buying cycles, balance storytelling with data, design experiments, track outcomes, and iterate rapidly to find what works.

Marketers at Canonical don’t just promote open source; they amplify it, ensuring our innovations reach the right people, at the right time, with clarity and purpose from developers to decision makers.

As a Marketing Associate , you won't just be watching from the sidelines; you will be hands-on from day one, collaborating with cross-functional teams to translate complex technical innovations into compelling market value.

Location: This role will be based remote globally

What Your Day Will Look Like


GTM Support: Contribute to go-to-market planning and execution. 
Storytelling: Draft and edit compelling messaging and positioning that highlights business value for diverse target audiences. 
Campaign Coordination: Assist in developing campaign plans and content dissemination strategies to ensure our message reaches the right people. 
Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partner with other demand generation and digital teams to help optimize marketing funnels and track campaign performance. 
Event & Social Support: Work with the wider marketing team to coordinate webinars, events, and social media promotions. 



What We Are Looking For In You


Tech Curiosity: A genuine passion for technology and a desire to learn about open source. 
Analytical Thinking: An ability to break down complex issues and translate them into clear, actionable recommendations. 
Collaboration: Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to build relationships within a fast-paced, cross-functional environment. 
Communication: Excellent verbal and written communication skills; you enjoy storytelling and creating impactful content. 
Proactive Mindset: A self-starter who can prioritize tasks effectively and meet deadlines in a dynamic setting. 
Foundational Knowledge: A basic understanding of marketing principles or go-to-market strategies (via your degree, internships, or personal projects). 
Growth Mindset: Someone who is eager to learn, open to feedback, and not afraid to take on new challenges. 



What We Offer You

Your base pay will depend on various factors including your geographical location, level of experience, knowledge and skills. In addition to the benefits above, certain roles are also eligible for additional benefits and rewards including annual bonuses and sales incentives based on revenue or utilisation. Our compensation philosophy is to ensure equity right across our global workforce.

In addition to a competitive base pay, we provide all team members with additional benefits, which reflect our values and ideals. Please note that additional benefits may apply depending on the work location and, for more information on these, please ask your Talent Partner.


Fully remote working environment - we’ve been working remotely since 2004!
Personal learning and development budget of 2,000USD per annum
Annual compensation review
Recognition rewards
Annual holiday leave
Parental Leave
Employee Assistance Programme
Opportunity to travel to new locations to meet colleagues at ‘sprints’
Priority Pass for travel and travel upgrades for long haul company events



About Canonical

Canonical is a pioneering tech firm that is at the forefront of the global move to open source. As the company that publishes Ubuntu, one of the most important open source projects and the platform for AI, IoT and the cloud, we are changing the world on a daily basis. We recruit on a global basis and set a very high standard for people joining the company. We expect excellence - in order to succeed, we need to be the best at what we do.

Canonical has been a remote-first company since its inception in 2004. Work at Canonical is a step into the future, and will challenge you to think differently, work smarter, learn new skills, and raise your game. Canonical provides a unique window into the world of 21st-century digital business.

Canonical is an equal opportunity employer

We are proud to foster a workplace free from discrimination. Diversity of experience, perspectives, and background create a better work environment and better products. Whatever your identity, we will give your application fair consideration.

description:
About the job

🎯 Buscamos Content Manager




No cualquiera. Alguien que entienda el poder de una buena historia y cómo contarla en cada plataforma.




📲 Redes sociales: Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, YouTube, Pinterest, LinkedIn, blogs, mailings.

✏ Estrategia y creatividad: keywords, storytelling y contenido que conecta.

We are looking to add a TikTok-first media buyer to our growing team at TopGrowthMarketing.com. This role is focused on paid ads via TikTok Ads Manager, but requires a solid understanding of the full TikTok ecosystem — including how TikTok Shop, Seller Center, and organic work — so you can collaborate effectively with our TikTok Shop team.

This is not a TikTok Shop/organic role — that side is handled by a dedicated team member. But you must understand how it works, what's possible vs. not possible on the seller/organic side, and how paid + organic can work together on TikTok.

Qualifications:

1+ years running paid ads in TikTok Ads Manager (required)
Experience with TikTok GMV Max campaigns — knowing when and how to use them
Strong understanding of the full TikTok ecosystem — the difference between TikTok Ads Manager, TikTok Business Center, TikTok Seller Center, TikTok Shop, and how they interact
Knows what's possible (and not possible) on the paid ads side so you can have informed conversations with the organic/shop team
Familiarity with TikTok Shop promotions, affiliate programs, and how they complement paid campaigns (you don't run these — but you need to know they exist and how they work)
Understanding of TikTok-specific creative performance — what makes content convert on TikTok vs. Meta
1+ years managing client accounts
Strong communication — you'll collaborate closely with our TikTok Shop specialist and creative team
Understanding of pixels, tracking, and attribution
Ability to deliver clear creative briefs based on ad performance data

Responsibilities:

Manage and optimize TikTok Ads Manager campaigns (prospecting, retargeting, GMV Max)
Brief the creative team on what's working — hooks, formats, angles — with TikTok-native insights
Collaborate with the TikTok Shop/organic team to align paid strategy with promotions, affiliate deals, and seller center activity
Proactively surface ideas for how paid + TikTok Shop can work together
Manage client communication and reporting
Continuously test, optimize, and document learnings

Bonus Points:

You've worked on or alongside a TikTok Shop team
You understand how TikTok Shop affiliates work and how paid can amplify creator/affiliate content
You know Cruva or similar TikTok organic management tools
What to know about this role: We have a dedicated TikTok Shop specialist who handles seller center, product listings, affiliate outreach, and shop setup. The biggest need is someone who speaks the same language — so when they bring ideas from the shop side, you're not starting from zero on how TikTok works. The goal is a real TikTok team that can collaborate and do more together.

title: Marketing & Growth Coordinator
skills: Develop and execute marketing initiatives designed to attract new clients and increase company visibility.
Support pipeline growth through outbound campaigns, targeted outreach, and brand awareness efforts.
Identify and engage industries, markets, and niches aligned with company growth goals.
Help generate inbound business opportunities through consistent and strategic marketing efforts.

Create and publish high-quality content that showcases company activity, hiring successes, operational updates, and client outcomes.
Develop a consistent flow of written, visual, and video-based content.

Help strengthen brand awareness and position the company as a trusted partner in the marketplace.

Help tell the company's story through content that attracts, engages, and converts prospects.
Work closely with leadership and internal teams to align marketing efforts with company goals and priorities.
Gather updates, success stories, wins, and content from internal departments.
Support company-wide growth initiatives through consistent execution and follow-through.

Proven experience in marketing, social media management, outbound marketing, lead generation, or related fields.

Strong written and verbal communication skills.
Confidence speaking with U.S.-based business owners and decision-makers.
Experience handling inbound inquiries and conducting initial prospect conversations.
Experience creating content across multiple platforms and formats.

description:
About the job

Great companies don’t grow because they post more content. They grow because the right people notice them.




Every day, potential clients are scrolling, searching, and deciding who they trust.




The challenge isn't getting attention—it's turning attention into conversations, opportunities, and growth.




That's why we're looking for a Marketing & Growth Coordinator who can help us expand our reach, strengthen our brand presence, and create meaningful connections with future clients.




This is a hands-on, execution-focused role that combines content creation, audience growth, outbound marketing, prospect engagement, and first-touch client communication. You'll play a direct role in helping us generate inbound and outbound opportunities while supporting the growth of our business pipeline.




If you're someone who enjoys turning ideas into action, creating engaging content, building relationships, and helping businesses grow, we'd love to meet you.




What You'll Do

Drive Marketing & Growth Initiatives

Develop and execute marketing initiatives designed to attract new clients and increase company visibility.
Support pipeline growth through outbound campaigns, targeted outreach, and brand awareness efforts.
Identify and engage industries, markets, and niches aligned with company growth goals.
Help generate inbound business opportunities through consistent and strategic marketing efforts.
Contribute directly to company growth by creating opportunities that lead to new client conversations.




Build and Grow Our Social Presence

Manage and maintain company social media platforms, including LinkedIn, Instagram, Facebook, and other relevant channels.
Create and publish high-quality content that showcases company activity, hiring successes, operational updates, and client outcomes.
Develop a consistent flow of written, visual, and video-based content.
Monitor engagement, interact with audiences when needed, and maintain an active online presence.
Help strengthen brand awareness and position the company as a trusted partner in the marketplace.




Execute Outbound & Inbound Marketing Efforts

Launch and manage outbound email and messaging campaigns tied to hiring activity, target industries, and company initiatives.
Build, organize, and maintain prospect outreach lists.
Handle inbound marketing inquiries and serve as the first point of contact for prospective clients.
Conduct first-touch discovery calls with potential clients.
Communicate professionally and confidently with U.S.-based business owners and decision-makers.




Create Content That Supports Growth

Develop marketing materials, including presentations, one-pagers, branded collateral, and other business development assets.
Coordinate simple video editing and content formatting.
Support company branding initiatives and ensure consistency across all platforms and materials.
Assist with branded materials, promotional items, and company marketing assets when needed.
Help tell the company's story through content that attracts, engages, and converts prospects.




Collaborate Across the Organization

Work closely with leadership and internal teams to align marketing efforts with company goals and priorities.
Gather updates, success stories, wins, and content from internal departments.
Support company-wide growth initiatives through consistent execution and follow-through.
Help ensure marketing efforts remain aligned with business objectives and growth targets.




What You Bring

Proven experience in marketing, social media management, outbound marketing, lead generation, or related fields.
Demonstrated success growing audiences, increasing engagement, generating leads, or supporting business development efforts.
Ability to showcase previous campaigns, audience growth results, content performance, or measurable marketing outcomes.
Strong written and verbal communication skills.
Confidence speaking with U.S.-based business owners and decision-makers.
Experience handling inbound inquiries and conducting initial prospect conversations.
Experience creating content across multiple platforms and formats.
Familiarity with social media management and outbound email marketing.
Basic video editing and content formatting skills are preferred.
Strong organizational skills and exceptional attention to detail.
Ability to manage multiple priorities and execute independently.
Comfortable working in a fast-paced environment where initiative and follow-through matter.




Why This Role Matters

Every marketing campaign, social post, outreach message, and prospect conversation creates an opportunity for growth.

In this role, you'll help shape how our company is seen, how new clients discover us, and how opportunities enter our pipeline. Your work will directly contribute to brand visibility, lead generation, client engagement, and long-term business growth.




If you're excited about combining marketing, content creation, outreach, and relationship-building into a role where your work creates measurable impact, we'd love to hear from you.

title: Creative Resource Executive

description:
About the job

At Jellyfish we believe that our individual differences are our greatest assets. We are passionate about empowering every employee to reach their full potential and contribute their unique perspectives to our collaborative environment. We are an equal opportunity employer and firmly believe that a diverse and inclusive workforce drives innovation and leads to better solutions. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, or any other personal characteristics that make them who they are.

Jellyfish is a global digital marketing agency; a unique fusion of tech enthusiasts, creative minds, and media and data experts all united to empower our clients along their digital journey. Our commitment to embracing diverse perspectives fuels our innovation and strategies that challenge the status quo, reinvent media activation, and craft influential stories for our global clients and their customers. Join us in shaping a future where business growth and personal fulfilment go hand in hand.

Job Description

Reporting into the Senior Resource Director, as a Resource Executive, you will play a crucial role in ensuring the effective utilization of our Creative team to meet business objectives. You will assist in developing workforce planning strategies, forecasting staffing needs, and optimizing resource allocation to improve operational efficiency. Collaborating closely with our capabilities, you will align workforce strategies with company goals, contribute to a positive work environment, and support the overall success of the organization.

Your Primary Responsibilities Include


Assist in the development and implementation of workforce planning strategies aligned with both short-term and long-term business goals.
Help the Resource Management team to identify, acquire, assess, and hire freelance candidates to fill open positions within a company.
Process documentation for freelance set-up to ensure a steam-lined freelance experience.
Helping new freelance employees integrate into the organization.
Process documentation for creator and contractor set-up as well as initiating invoices and payments.
Conduct regular assessments of staffing needs based on current and projected workloads, industry trends, and company growth.
Utilize data analytics and forecasting tools to support staffing predictions and improve resource allocation.
Collaborate with department managers, People team, Integrated Producers and other team members to understand staffing requirements and ensure alignment across teams.
Help facilitate communication across departments to ensure a unified approach to workforce planning.
Evaluate team capacities and suggest areas for improvement to ensure optimal resource utilization.
Work with hiring managers to address skill gaps and explore upskilling opportunities.
Adjusting strategies as needed to meet changing business requirements.
Track and report workforce metrics to leadership, highlighting areas for improvement and talent risk management.
Stay informed on industry trends, emerging technologies, and best practices in workforce planning.
Continuously assess and improve workforce planning processes to adapt to evolving needs.
Support the global Resource Team in building a well-resourced, effective, and productive global team.


Qualifications


1+ years of experience in workforce planning, capacity planning, or a related role within a digital marketing, advertising, or technology business.
Advanced English proficiency.
Experience using Wrike or similar software for resource and workforce management.
Proficiency in data analysis and forecasting tools.
Strong ability to collaborate across teams and departments to optimize resources.
Familiarity with digital marketing processes and workflows, particularly within creative teams.
Basic understanding of project management principles and practices.
Able to take direction and follow guidance given from other team members and the leadership team.


Note: We emphasise skills, expertise and behavioural attributes over years of experience and traditional degrees. If you want to join our collaborative team, we invite you to apply today with your resume in English.

Additional Information

Join Jellyfish and experience a workplace where we prioritise your growth, celebrate your contributions, and empower you to tailor your work environment to suit your needs.

💰 Savings fund: You'll save 5% of your monthly income and Jellyfish will contribute the same amount you save to your savings fund.

💫 Custom Work Environment: Work remotely for up to 60% of your days and shape your day between 8am. and 6:30pm with flexible working hours.

🏥 Health Insurance: We provide a private health insurance policy that includes visual and dental plan coverage.

Unfortunately, there has been an increase in fake recruiters impersonating Jellyfish and unlawfully using our brand name. If you are unsure if an email with a job offer you have received is genuinely from Jellyfish, or if you suspect any fraudulent activity, please report it to talentacquisition@jellyfish.com.

title: Vendor Solutions Consultant, Google Cloud
skills: Bachelor’s degree or equivalent practical experience.
8 years of experience in a technical project management or a customer-facing role.
8 years of experience in transformation and consulting within engineering services and AI data workflows.
5 years of experience leading vendor strategy and operations for software development projects, at a technology company.
Experience in vendor managment/operations.
Experience in translating AI advancements and intelligent automation capabilities into engineering vendor solutions and commercial models (e.g., pay per unit (PPU), managed services).
Knowledge of engineering services and the AI landscape, including AI data operations workflows like Human-in-the-Loop (HiTL), RLHF, and red-teaming.
Ability to build relationships with clients and act as a trusted advisor and partner.
Ability to communicate and collaborate cross-functionally and at the executive level.
Excellent organizational and project management skills.
Use market research to benchmark costs and streamline agreement life-cycles, enhancing overall operational efficiency. Create detailed service requirements and summary reports.
Design custom solutions covering location and commercial terms to maximize value and adhere to industry best practices.
Lead Cloud vendor strategy including sourcing and contracting. Embed AI as a core component of solutions.
Develop scalable, cost-optimal operating models. Manage the overall relationship and drive influence with the business teams.

Manage or work within AI data operations workflows, including human-in-the-loop (HiTL), model evaluations (RLHF/supervised fine-tuning (SFT), and red-teaming/safety annotation. Execute vendor strategy and contracting specifically for Cloud Engineering and AI Solutions requirements.
description:
About the job

Please submit your resume in English - we can only consider applications submitted in this language.

Only applications of candidates with Mexican citizenship will be evaluated for this role in compliance with the provisions of Article 7 of the Federal Labor Law.

Note: Google's hybrid workplace includes remote and in-office roles. By applying to this position you will have an opportunity to share your preferred working location from the following:

In-office locations: Buenos Aires, Argentina.

Remote location(s): Mexico; Brazil; Colombia.Minimum qualifications:


Bachelor’s degree or equivalent practical experience.
8 years of experience in a technical project management or a customer-facing role. 
8 years of experience in transformation and consulting within engineering services and AI data workflows.
5 years of experience leading vendor strategy and operations for software development projects, at a technology company.
Experience in vendor managment/operations.


Preferred qualifications:


Experience in translating AI advancements and intelligent automation capabilities into engineering vendor solutions and commercial models (e.g., pay per unit (PPU), managed services).
Knowledge of engineering services and the AI landscape, including AI data operations workflows like Human-in-the-Loop (HiTL), RLHF, and red-teaming.
Ability to build relationships with clients and act as a trusted advisor and partner.
Ability to communicate and collaborate cross-functionally and at the executive level.
Excellent organizational and project management skills.


About The Job

The Google Cloud Platform team helps customers transform and build what's next for their business — all with technology built in the cloud. Our products are developed for security, reliability and scalability, running the full stack from infrastructure to applications to devices and hardware. Our teams are dedicated to helping our customers — developers, small and large businesses, educational institutions and government agencies — see the benefits of our technology come to life. As part of an entrepreneurial team in this rapidly growing business, you will play a key role in understanding the needs of our customers and help shape the future of businesses of all sizes use technology to connect with customers, employees and partners.

In this role, you will act as a advisor to Google Cloud leadership, supporting the implementation of the Vendor Management Office (VMO) roadmap. You will have knowledge in transformation consulting, alongside core engineering services and the AI Data operations sector, including reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) and human-in-the-loop (HiTL).

You will be tasked with managing vendor strategy and procurement for AI and Cloud Engineering, and will create efficient, scalable operating structures while overseeing compliance and operational safety. By fostering an AI-First approach, you will adapt to technological shifts and cultivate performance-driven relationships with global Strategy, AI, and engineering teams. You will also be involved in designing transformative sourcing initiatives, establishing governance framework, and ensuring rigorous agreement adherence in specialized domains like safety red-teaming.

Google Cloud accelerates every organization’s ability to digitally transform its business and industry. We deliver enterprise-grade solutions that leverage Google’s cutting-edge technology, and tools that help developers build more sustainably. Customers in more than 200 countries and territories turn to Google Cloud as their trusted partner to enable growth and solve their most critical business problems.

Responsibilities


Use market research to benchmark costs and streamline agreement life-cycles, enhancing overall operational efficiency. Create detailed service requirements and summary reports.
Design custom solutions covering location and commercial terms to maximize value and adhere to industry best practices.
Lead Cloud vendor strategy including sourcing and contracting. Embed AI as a core component of solutions.
Develop scalable, cost-optimal operating models. Manage the overall relationship and drive influence with the business teams.
Manage or work within AI data operations workflows, including human-in-the-loop (HiTL), model evaluations (RLHF/supervised fine-tuning (SFT), and red-teaming/safety annotation. Execute vendor strategy and contracting specifically for Cloud Engineering and AI Solutions requirements.



Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form .

title: Customer Success Renewals Specialist – Support Contracts (North America)
skills: You will manage a defined portfolio of accounts with a primary focus on renewing support contracts on time and in full.
You will engage customers and channel partners ahead of contract expiration to create seamless renewal outcomes. Sending notifications of renewal expiration to customers.
You will communicate contract coverage, renewal options, timelines, and the value of continued support services.
You will ensure renewals, working toward renewal rate, churn, and forecasting Indicators set by leadership.
You will use Salesforce to manage renewal opportunities, track progress, maintain accurate contract data, receive and send customer quotes, and support forecasting activities.
You will partner with Partner Success Managers to align renewal strategies with channel partners.
You will collaborate with Sales, Channel teams, Contracts, and Operations on pricing, terms, documentation, and renewal execution.
You will provide first-line support for renewal-related inquiries, including contract scope, pricing questions, and renewal logistics.
You will identify renewal risk early and escalate to ensure leadership visibility and proactive mitigation.
You will follow established Salesforce and renewal processes to ensure data quality, compliance, and operational efficiency.
You will support our revenue retention and growth strategy by reducing renewal friction and improving the customer experience.
Bachelor's degree.
Minimum 1–2 years of experience in Renewals, Customer Success, Inside Sales, Sales Operations, or Contract Management (preferred).
Experience working with CRM tools such as Salesforce for opportunity management, forecasting, and reporting.
Experience supporting North America–based customers.
