Apply Now!

title: Operations Manager (Amazon FBA) - Remote
skills: Directly manage our inventory and operations lead, our graphic designer, and our PPC team. Four people now, growing to six to eight within 12 months.
Run weekly 1-on-1s. Give direct feedback regularly, not only when something breaks.
Own performance management. Catch underperformance early and address it directly.
Reinforce a culture where people own their results and do not escalate every decision.
Run the weekly team meeting and the monthly all-hands. Prep the agenda, facilitate, capture action items in Asana, follow up to completion.
Own Asana as the single source of truth. Nothing falls through the cracks.
Deliver a weekly ops report to me: team performance, project status, inventory, account health, financials, and blockers.
Review and approve all reorder recommendations before POs go to suppliers.
Verify sales velocity, lead times, safety stock, and FBA receiving timelines.
Own the re-order pipeline: production, freight, FBA inbound, and 3PL coordination.
Quality-check the operations lead's work. Catch errors before they become stockouts.
Run the weekly inventory review: stock levels, inbound shipments, reorder status, and flags.
Build and own our SOPs, dashboards, and Notion workspace as a centralized knowledge and reporting hub. Keep it current.
Own the software stack, vendor relationships, and recurring subscriptions.
Final sign-off on supplier invoices and operational spend.
Own the monitoring and protection of our Amazon account health on a daily basis
Track key account health metrics: Order Defect Rate, Late Shipment Rate, policy violations, IP complaints, and listing suppressions
When issues arise (listing removals, category disputes, compliance notifications), you take the lead: gather documentation, open and escalate cases, and coordinate with our team to resolve
Proactively audit listings for compliance risks (product claims, image requirements, restricted keywords, category accuracy) before Amazon flags them
Monitor and resolve stranded inventory, suppressed listings, and any FBA-related account issues
Lead the hiring process for new roles: JDs, screening, interviewing.
Onboard new hires with structured 30/60/90 day plans, tool access, and training docs.
Build the playbook so the next five hires are easier than the last five.
Run the weekly PPC check-in. Collect reports, summarize the data, flag anomalies, hold the cadence.
You own the rhythm and the accountability around PPC. You do not own the strategy.
Primary point of contact for all external consultants and contractors
Follow up aggressively on timelines and deliverables
Flag delays with options, not just problems
You are the first line of accountability for daily execution. If something is late, unclear, or below standard, you address it before it reaches me.
You are the operational filter between the team and me. Resolve at your level. Escalate only what is strategic or exceeds your authority.
Hands-on supplier negotiation, sourcing, and freight booking. Our operations lead executes these. You own the oversight, the reorder approval, and the final sign-off.
PPC strategy and execution. Our PPC Lead owns ad performance.
Compensation: $4-5k/month depending on experience. Independent contractor.
Performance bonus: Quarterly performance bonus introduced at month 6 tied to a clear scorecard, inventory and operational KPIs, team execution, and company growth milestones.
Structure: 90-day probationary period with structured reviews at 30, 60, and 90 days. Clear milestones, clear feedback, no guessing where you stand.
Growth: Comp grows as the company grows. We hit milestones, you hit milestones.
Setup: Fully remote, flexible within the AST overlap window.
description:
About the job

𝗢𝗽𝗲𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻𝘀 𝗠𝗮𝗻𝗮𝗴𝗲𝗿 - 𝗔𝗺𝗮𝘇𝗼𝗻 𝗙𝗕𝗔 𝗕𝗲𝗮𝘂𝘁𝘆 𝗕𝗿𝗮𝗻𝗱 - 𝗦𝗰𝗮𝗹𝗲 𝗨𝘀 𝘁𝗼 𝟴 𝗙𝗶𝗴𝘂𝗿𝗲𝘀




This is a full-time role (40 hours per week).




𝗦𝗧𝗬𝗟𝗜𝗔 // 𝗖𝗢𝗥𝗘 𝗩𝗔𝗟𝗨𝗘𝗦

These aren't words on a wall. These are the operating system of our company. They define who we hire, who we promote, who we part ways with, and how we make decisions every single day.




1 EXTREME OWNERSHIP: We own everything in our world. No excuses. No finger-pointing. No waiting to be told twice.

2 GROW OR GO: We grow, or we make room for someone who will. Complacency is the enemy.

3 BIAS FOR ACTION: When in doubt, move. 80% of the information is enough.

4 SPEED WINS: Fast beats perfect. Always. Tasks completed ahead of deadline, not on or after.

5 MULTIPLY: We use AI, automation, and systems to multiply our effort and outpace competitors.

6 INNOVATION: We don't copy. We create. The standard is "is this different, and is it better?"




If these values don't describe how you already operate, we wish you the best of luck finding the right fit somewhere else.




If they do, keep reading.




𝗪𝗛𝗢 𝗪𝗘 𝗔𝗥𝗘




𝗢𝘂𝗿 𝗠𝗶𝘀𝘀𝗶𝗼𝗻: To build the most dominant beauty brand on Amazon through better products, faster execution, and a team that refuses to lose.




We're a 7 year old beauty brand that started on Amazon. We specialize in chin masks and eye masks. We're a mid-7 figure brand and on our way to scaling to 8 figures. Our customers love our products, our growth is real, and we need someone to run the operational machine while I focus on building the brand and developing new products.




I'm the founder and CEO. Right now I'm spending an inordinate amount of time every week doing things I shouldn't be doing. Chasing updates. Running meetings. Reviewing invoices. Writing SOPs. Troubleshooting tools. Managing my team's daily output. That needs to stop. Not because I can't do it, but because it's not the highest-value use of my time anymore.




That's where you come in!




𝗧𝗛𝗘 𝗥𝗢𝗟𝗘

You'll be the #2 at this company. Not on paper. For real. You'll own every operational system, every process, every meeting, every deadline, and the daily output of your direct reports. I set the direction. You build and run the machine.

Within 90 days, I should be able to step away from operations entirely and focus on growth, product development, and strategy. You make that possible by actually owning it, not by checking boxes and waiting for me to tell you what to do next.




𝗪𝗛𝗔𝗧 𝗬𝗢𝗨'𝗟𝗟 𝗔𝗖𝗧𝗨𝗔𝗟𝗟𝗬 𝗗𝗢




The Team




Directly manage our inventory and operations lead, our graphic designer, and our PPC team. Four people now, growing to six to eight within 12 months.
Run weekly 1-on-1s. Give direct feedback regularly, not only when something breaks.
Own performance management. Catch underperformance early and address it directly.
Reinforce a culture where people own their results and do not escalate every decision.




The Operating Cadence




Run the weekly team meeting and the monthly all-hands. Prep the agenda, facilitate, capture action items in Asana, follow up to completion.
Own Asana as the single source of truth. Nothing falls through the cracks.
Deliver a weekly ops report to me: team performance, project status, inventory, account health, financials, and blockers.




Inventory and Supply Chain 




Review and approve all reorder recommendations before POs go to suppliers.
Verify sales velocity, lead times, safety stock, and FBA receiving timelines.
Own the re-order pipeline: production, freight, FBA inbound, and 3PL coordination.
Quality-check the operations lead's work. Catch errors before they become stockouts.
Run the weekly inventory review: stock levels, inbound shipments, reorder status, and flags.




Systems and Knowledge Infrastructure




Build and own our SOPs, dashboards, and Notion workspace as a centralized knowledge and reporting hub. Keep it current.
Own the software stack, vendor relationships, and recurring subscriptions.
Final sign-off on supplier invoices and operational spend.




Account Health 




Own the monitoring and protection of our Amazon account health on a daily basis
Track key account health metrics: Order Defect Rate, Late Shipment Rate, policy violations, IP complaints, and listing suppressions
When issues arise (listing removals, category disputes, compliance notifications), you take the lead: gather documentation, open and escalate cases, and coordinate with our team to resolve
Proactively audit listings for compliance risks (product claims, image requirements, restricted keywords, category accuracy) before Amazon flags them
Monitor and resolve stranded inventory, suppressed listings, and any FBA-related account issues







Hiring and Onboarding




Lead the hiring process for new roles: JDs, screening, interviewing.
Onboard new hires with structured 30/60/90 day plans, tool access, and training docs.
Build the playbook so the next five hires are easier than the last five.




PPC Operational Rhythm




Run the weekly PPC check-in. Collect reports, summarize the data, flag anomalies, hold the cadence.
You own the rhythm and the accountability around PPC. You do not own the strategy.




Consultant and Contractor Management

Primary point of contact for all external consultants and contractors
Follow up aggressively on timelines and deliverables
Flag delays with options, not just problems




The Filter




You are the first line of accountability for daily execution. If something is late, unclear, or below standard, you address it before it reaches me.
You are the operational filter between the team and me. Resolve at your level. Escalate only what is strategic or exceeds your authority.




WHAT YOU DON'T OWN

Hands-on supplier negotiation, sourcing, and freight booking. Our operations lead executes these. You own the oversight, the reorder approval, and the final sign-off.
PPC strategy and execution. Our PPC Lead owns ad performance.




You partner with these people. You do not run their craft. You run the machine around them.




𝗪𝗛𝗔𝗧'𝗦 𝗛𝗔𝗥𝗗 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗝𝗢𝗕

I'm not going to sugarcoat it.

You're stepping into a small team that has only ever reported directly to me. Adding a management layer is a culture shift. You'll need to earn trust. It won't be given automatically.




You'll need to set standards and enforce them without breaking the relationship.




You'll need to manage creative talent firmly without killing their motivation.




I'm deeply invested in this business, and early on I'll be more involved in your work than either of us would like. I need someone strong enough to earn that trust quickly and push back when I'm in your lane. If you're someone who just says yes to everything the boss says, you'll fail here.




We make decisions on 80% information and adjust as we go. If you need everything perfect before you act, this isn't the right fit.




This is a demanding role. You're managing people, projects, systems, vendors, reporting and finances all at once. If you want a predictable 9-to-5 where you do the same thing every day, this isn't it.




𝗪𝗛𝗔𝗧'𝗦 𝗚𝗥𝗘𝗔𝗧 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗝𝗢𝗕

You're the #2 at a real, profitable, growing brand. Not employee #347 at a faceless company.

The operational infrastructure you build directly increases the value of this company. You're building something that matters.




I'm direct, I give clear feedback, and I'm invested in your success. You'll always know where you stand.




Full remote. Must be available during core hours (10 AM to 3 PM AST). No commute, no office politics.




You'll build systems from the ground up. If you love creating order from chaos, this is your playground.




This role grows. As we scale to 8 figures, so does your responsibility and your impact.




𝗥𝗘𝗤𝗨𝗜𝗥𝗘𝗠𝗘𝗡𝗧𝗦 (𝗻𝗼𝗻-𝗻𝗲𝗴𝗼𝘁𝗶𝗮𝗯𝗹𝗲)

-3+ years managing operations for an Amazon FBA brand ($3M+ annual revenue)

-Managed at least 3 remote team members directly

-Advanced daily user of AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT) with specific, demonstrable examples

-English fluency. This is a writing-heavy role and your communication must be crystal clear.

-Available during Atlantic Standard Time hours (10 AM to 3 PM AST / UTC-4)

-Built SOPs and operational systems from scratch

-Proficient in Amazon Seller Central, Asana (or equivalent), and Excel/Sheets







COMPENSATION

Compensation: $4-5k/month depending on experience. Independent contractor.
Performance bonus: Quarterly performance bonus introduced at month 6 tied to a clear scorecard, inventory and operational KPIs, team execution, and company growth milestones.
Structure: 90-day probationary period with structured reviews at 30, 60, and 90 days. Clear milestones, clear feedback, no guessing where you stand.
Growth: Comp grows as the company grows. We hit milestones, you hit milestones.
Setup: Fully remote, flexible within the AST overlap window.




This is a gross figure. You are responsible for tax procedures in your country of residence. Apply only if you are happy to work as an independent contractor.




𝗛𝗢𝗪 𝗧𝗢 𝗔𝗣𝗣𝗟𝗬

Apply here: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSe_jnFKzbkzMeSAUzaZNSqVTCesn6lmIU-4gV0ei3F5UsxScA/viewform?usp=header




You'll be asked to upload your resume, answer a few short questions, and submit a brief written response.




Applications without both a resume and written response will not be reviewed.




This is a full-time role, 40 hours per week. Our team works async, so you manage most of your own schedule. The one fixed requirement is a daily core overlap from 10:00 AM to 3:00 PM AST (UTC-4), Monday to Friday, for meetings and real-time collaboration.

title: Senior Data Analyst
skills: Leverage complex datasets to identify and quantify key performance indicators.
Create dashboards and data visualizations that monitor & measure success.
Establish and own best practices & training materials for data-savvy users within functional departments.
Be a key leader in championing the impact of data, company-wide.
5+ years in a Data Analyst or similar role
Expert-level proficiency in SQL and using it to create derivative data structures
Experience with a data visualization tool (Sigma knowledge is preferred; Tableau or Looker is also a plus)
Proven track record of using data to drive significant transformations in business processes
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, including the ability to distill complex ideas and present them with influence to non-data focused individuals and leadership.
Past experience in a SaaS business model is preferred
Experience using dbt/Python/R/Git is a plus
Proficient in AI model implementation or highly motivated to develop expertise in the field
description:
About the job

ABOUT THE COMPANY:

Clari + Salesloft are building the next era of enterprise revenue — one where teams make confident decisions powered by AI and real signals. By combining our scale, insights, and AI innovation, we’re building the industry’s first Predictive Revenue System, enabling humans and AI to work together to make smarter decisions and drive consistent growth.

With thousands of customers using our platforms every day, we have an unmatched view into how revenue is actually won — the Revenue Context that reveals what happens, when, and with what outcome. This gives us a unique opportunity to transform an entire category and set a new benchmark for how modern revenue teams operate.

Join us to help transform how companies around the world run revenue — and build the platform that will guide leading revenue teams into the future.

THE OPPORTUNITY:

At Clari + Salesloft, our Senior Data Analysts are pivotal to our company's success. You will be a leader within our fast-growing and high-performing Data & Analytics team, responsible for driving departments to monitor their performance, understand trends, and make more informed decisions.

On a day-to-day basis, you will be responsible for owning the delivery of actionable insights that drive positive change across the business. Specifically, you will:


Leverage complex datasets to identify and quantify key performance indicators. 
Create dashboards and data visualizations that monitor & measure success. 
Establish and own best practices & training materials for data-savvy users within functional departments. 
Be a key leader in championing the impact of data, company-wide.


In addition to working with amazing colleagues who exemplify our ‘team over self’ core value, you will also have the opportunity to [EXAMPLE: sell an impactful and revolutionary software that is changing the way sellers sell.] You will have an opportunity to make a difference.

WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR:

We are seeking a highly experienced, results-oriented Senior Data Analyst who can empower the business to make better decisions with data. We are interested in people who are equal parts analytically focused and business-driven, motivated by solving real-world problems and exploring new approaches.

If you’re looking for an opportunity to learn more, do more, and become more, then becoming a Senior Data Analyst is the career path for you!

THE TEAM:

Our Clari + Salesloft Data & Analytics team is composed of Data Analysts & Data Engineers who are all aligned on one mission: to redefine the Sales Engagement space and activate the authentic seller in all of us.

The Data & Analytics team consists of passionate data engineers and analysts with a drive to enable Salesloft to make better decisions. Our team shares a few common traits: they are accountable, unafraid to experiment, and continuously learning.

THE SKILL SET:


5+ years in a Data Analyst or similar role
Expert-level proficiency in SQL and using it to create derivative data structures
Experience with a data visualization tool (Sigma knowledge is preferred; Tableau or Looker is also a plus)
Proven track record of using data to drive significant transformations in business processes
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, including the ability to distill complex ideas and present them with influence to non-data focused individuals and leadership.
Past experience in a SaaS business model is preferred
Experience using dbt/Python/R/Git is a plus
Proficient in AI model implementation or highly motivated to develop expertise in the field


At Clari + Salesloft, we are committed to creating an inclusive and supportive workplace where everyone belongs and can thrive. We focus on culture add, not culture fit, and believe our teams are made stronger by the unique perspectives, experiences, and identities each person brings.

We are proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer and provide employment opportunities to all employees and applicants without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, pregnancy, or any other characteristic protected by law.

If you’re excited about this role even though your experience may not perfectly match every requirement, we encourage you to apply. We are actively hiring across multiple geographies and would love to welcome passionate, curious, and mission-driven individuals to our growing team. Explore our open roles and consider joining us!

Please note that all official communication regarding job opportunities at Clari + Salesloft will come from an @clari.com or @ salesloft.com email address. If you receive messages on LinkedIn or other job platforms claiming to be from Clari + Salesloft, they may not be legitimate. To verify the authenticity of any job-related communication, please visit our official Careers Page.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, notetaking, or summarizing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment — all hiring decisions are made by people. If you would like more information about how your data is processed or prefer to opt out of any AI-assisted tools, please let your recruiter know. Opting out will not impact your experience or consideration.

title: Brand Campaign Operations Sr. Analyst
skills: Manage the overall Brand Campaigns and Partnerships budget, including tracking multi-year commitments and ensuring alignment with Finance.
Partner with Finance on budget reconciliations, requarterization, and scenario planning to maintain financial accuracy and flexibility.
Support the Brand and Partnerships team with quick turn and executive approvals. Drive rigor around budget submissions and invoicing deadlines.
Lead the team's strategic planning framework process (V2MOM creation or similar), driving the definition and documentation of annual targets.
Own the monthly status updates and performance pacing process to ensure team targets are accurately tracked and on schedule.
Maintain budget and milestone alignment with all Brand Campaigns and Partnerships work and campaign/media delivery timelines.
Provide bi-weekly executive-level updates with key dependencies, risks, and critical operational callouts to leadership.
Provide consolidated operational reports for leadership, including comprehensive views of budget status, target pacing, and resource commitments.
Serve as the strategic business operations focus, ensuring budgets, targets, and pacing are accurate, well-communicated, and fully aligned with both leadership and Finance partners.
Serve as the team's primary technical resource for internal AI tooling, agent building, and prompt engineering.

Design, build, and iterate on AI agents and automated workflows that streamline repetitive operational tasks, improve team efficiency, and reduce manual lift across budget tracking, reporting, and cross-functional coordination.
Partner with team members to identify high-impact opportunities where AI tools can accelerate work, and translate those opportunities into practical, deployed solutions.
Develop and maintain a library of reusable prompts, tools, and AI-powered workflows tailored to the team's operational needs.
5+ years of progressive experience in marketing operations, finance, analytics, or a similar business operations role, preferably within a fast-paced environment.
Proven expertise in budget management, financial tracking, and operational reporting.
Advanced Excel skills required; demonstrated ability to handle large datasets with a detail-oriented approach.
Demonstrated success in driving operational processes from conception to completion, with a strong focus on measurable business outcomes.
Strong verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to articulate complex financial and operational information clearly and concisely to diverse stakeholders.
Highly analytical and data-driven, with the ability to translate insights into actionable operational strategies.
Proactive in identifying potential risks or dependencies and developing creative solutions in a dynamic environment.
Hands-on experience building AI agents, custom GPTs, or LLM-powered tools using platforms such as ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Agentforce, or similar.
Proficiency in prompt engineering, with the ability to design effective prompts for a range of operational use cases.
Familiarity with AI workflow and automation tools (e.g., Slack AI) to build scalable, team-facing solutions.
Comfortable working at the intersection of technology and operations — able to independently scope, build, and deploy lightweight AI-powered tools without deep engineering support.
Familiarity with marketing campaign delivery timelines.
Experience working closely with Finance teams on budget forecasting and reconciliation.
Experience using or building AI agents within enterprise platforms (e.g., Salesforce Agentforce)
Prior experience acting as an AI champion or internal resource for a non-technical team, democratizing access to AI tools and capabilities.
description:
About the job

To get the best candidate experience, please consider applying for a maximum of 3 roles within 12 months to ensure you are not duplicating efforts.

Job Category

Marketing & Communications

Job Details

About Salesforce

Salesforce is the #1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all.

Ready to level-up your career at the company leading workforce transformation in the agentic era? You’re in the right place! Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce.

We are seeking a highly motivated, results-oriented, and experienced Marketing Strategy and Planning Manager to serve as the financial and operational backbone for the Brand Campaigns and Partnerships team. This role is central to driving operational excellence by ensuring rigorous budget management, accurate target setting, and effective annual performance pacing for high-impact brand initiatives.

Responsibilities

Budget Management & Financial Oversight:


Manage the overall Brand Campaigns and Partnerships budget, including tracking multi-year commitments and ensuring alignment with Finance.
Partner with Finance on budget reconciliations, requarterization, and scenario planning to maintain financial accuracy and flexibility.
Support the Brand and Partnerships team with quick turn and executive approvals. Drive rigor around budget submissions and invoicing deadlines.


Annual Targets & Performance Pacing


Lead the team's strategic planning framework process (V2MOM creation or similar), driving the definition and documentation of annual targets.
Own the monthly status updates and performance pacing process to ensure team targets are accurately tracked and on schedule.


Timeline & Leadership Updates


Maintain budget and milestone alignment with all Brand Campaigns and Partnerships work and campaign/media delivery timelines.
Provide bi-weekly executive-level updates with key dependencies, risks, and critical operational callouts to leadership.


Reporting & Strategic Business Operations


Provide consolidated operational reports for leadership, including comprehensive views of budget status, target pacing, and resource commitments.
Serve as the strategic business operations focus, ensuring budgets, targets, and pacing are accurate, well-communicated, and fully aligned with both leadership and Finance partners.


AI Tooling & Intelligent Operations


Serve as the team's primary technical resource for internal AI tooling, agent building, and prompt engineering.
Design, build, and iterate on AI agents and automated workflows that streamline repetitive operational tasks, improve team efficiency, and reduce manual lift across budget tracking, reporting, and cross-functional coordination.
Partner with team members to identify high-impact opportunities where AI tools can accelerate work, and translate those opportunities into practical, deployed solutions.
Develop and maintain a library of reusable prompts, tools, and AI-powered workflows tailored to the team's operational needs.


Experience / Skills Required


5+ years of progressive experience in marketing operations, finance, analytics, or a similar business operations role, preferably within a fast-paced environment.
Proven expertise in budget management, financial tracking, and operational reporting.
Advanced Excel skills required; demonstrated ability to handle large datasets with a detail-oriented approach.
Demonstrated success in driving operational processes from conception to completion, with a strong focus on measurable business outcomes.
Strong verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to articulate complex financial and operational information clearly and concisely to diverse stakeholders.
Highly analytical and data-driven, with the ability to translate insights into actionable operational strategies.
Proactive in identifying potential risks or dependencies and developing creative solutions in a dynamic environment.
Hands-on experience building AI agents, custom GPTs, or LLM-powered tools using platforms such as ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Agentforce, or similar.
Proficiency in prompt engineering, with the ability to design effective prompts for a range of operational use cases.
Familiarity with AI workflow and automation tools (e.g., Slack AI) to build scalable, team-facing solutions.
Comfortable working at the intersection of technology and operations — able to independently scope, build, and deploy lightweight AI-powered tools without deep engineering support.


Preferred Qualifications


Familiarity with marketing campaign delivery timelines.
Experience working closely with Finance teams on budget forecasting and reconciliation.
Experience using or building AI agents within enterprise platforms (e.g., Salesforce Agentforce)
Prior experience acting as an AI champion or internal resource for a non-technical team, democratizing access to AI tools and capabilities.


Unleash Your Potential

When you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and be your best, and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can do your best. Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. Apply today to not only shape the future — but to redefine what’s possible — for yourself, for AI, and the world.

Accommodations

If you need a reasonable accommodation during the application or the recruiting process, please submit a request via this Accommodations Request Form.

Please note that Salesforce uses artificial intelligence (AI) tools to help our recruiters assess and evaluate candidates’ resumes and qualifications throughout the recruiting process. Humans will always make any candidate selection and hiring decisions. Please see our Candidate Privacy Statement for more information about how we use your personal data and your rights, including with regard to use of AI tools and opt out options.

Posting Statement

Salesforce is an equal opportunity employer and maintains a policy of non-discrimination with all employees and applicants for employment. What does that mean exactly? It means that at Salesforce, we believe in equality for all. And we believe we can lead the path to equality in part by creating a workplace that’s inclusive, and free from discrimination. Know your rights: workplace discrimination is illegal. Any employee or potential employee will be assessed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications – without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender expression or identity, transgender status, age, disability, veteran or marital status, political viewpoint, or other classifications protected by law. This policy applies to current and prospective employees, no matter where they are in their Salesforce employment journey. It also applies to recruiting, hiring, job assignment, compensation, promotion, benefits, training, assessment of job performance, discipline, termination, and everything in between. Recruiting, hiring, and promotion decisions at Salesforce are fair and based on merit. The same goes for compensation, benefits, promotions, transfers, reduction in workforce, recall, training, and education.

title: Executive Assistant
skills: Own the founder's inbox: triage, draft responses, surface what needs attention, and clear what doesn't.
Own the founder's calendar: schedule, reschedule, defend protected time, and prepare daily briefings.
Take detailed notes in meetings and convert them into tracked action items.
Hold the founder accountable to what she commits to in meetings, proactively remind her of deadlines, deliverables, and outstanding promises.
Anticipate needs and surface issues before they become problems.
Be the primary point of contact with vendors and service providers (electricity, internet, catering, accounting, software providers, and others).
Pull basic reports from Zenoti and other systems as requested; problem-solve to figure out how to get the data needed.
Send communications to clients through Zenoti CRM and other tools.
Organize scattered or undocumented SOPs into clean, actionable workflows.
Provide timely reminders to the founder for bills, payroll, accounting deliverables, and recurring obligations.
Draft and send communications to clients and employees as directed by the founder.
Follow up on outstanding action items from team meetings until they are resolved.
Escalate issues that need the founder's attention with proposed solutions, not just problems.
Blended Personal Support: 10% Role Allocation
Handle personal tasks that intersect with work, exercised with strong discretion and confidentiality.
5+ years as an Executive Assistant, Chief of Staff, or senior administrative professional supporting a founder, CEO, or senior executive.
1+ year of remote work experience supporting clients in North America, Europe, Australia, or the UAE.
Experience supporting a Founder or Executive in a Beauty, Wellness, or Lifestyle brand.
Strong written and spoken English that is clear, professional, and confident in vendor and client communication.
Native-level fluency is not required; capability and experience are the priority.
Proven track record of working autonomously under ambiguity, taking scattered direction, and producing organized results.
Demonstrated ability to manage vendor relationships and external communications independently.
Experience working in environments where the founder/executive has very limited time to direct day-to-day work.
Strong written communication, comfortable drafting on behalf of an executive.
High discretion and confidentiality in this role have access to financial, personal, and sensitive business information.
Google Workspace (Gmail, Calendar, Drive, Docs, Sheets) - Fluency Required.
Slack and/or Microsoft Teams - Fluency Required.
Project Management Tools (Asana, Notion, ClickUp, or similar) - Fluency in at least one required.
Zenoti (Salon Management System) - experience preferred, but the ability to learn quickly is the actual requirement.
Shopify, Qnity, Accounting Platforms (QuickBooks or similar) - Exposure is a Plus.
Minimum 100 Mbps Internet Speed
Windows 11 or macOS 15 Sequoia
Minimum Intel i5 8th gen with 8GB RAM or Apple M1 chip with 8GB RAM
Webcam and Headset
Designated Office Space in your home
Familiarity with account handover and access management is a plus.
Exposure to financial systems or reporting tools like Qnity.
Takes initiative and moves projects forward without waiting for direction, proactively identifying priorities and getting things done.
Communicates clearly, concisely, and confidently, with the ability to ask thoughtful questions and push back when necessary.
Maintains exceptional attention to detail, ensuring every commitment, follow-up, and task is tracked through completion.
Thrives in ambiguity, able to navigate unfamiliar situations, solve problems independently, and determine the best path forward.
Learns new systems and tools quickly, adapting with minimal guidance.
Handles sensitive business and personal information with the highest level of professionalism, discretion, and confidentiality.
description:
About the job

Role: Executive Assistant (Experience in the wellness, salon, or spa industry is a must-have)

Contract: Full-time Remote Contractor (40 hours weekly / 160 hours monthly)

Business Hours: Monday to Friday, 8 AM to 5 PM EST

Rate: $2,400 - $3,000 USD monthly (salary is negotiable based on experience and location)

Availability to Start: Immediately




Client's Profile

Founded in New Jersey in 1997, this award-winning luxury salon and wellness spa is more than a destination for beauty; it's a space for transformation, self-expression, and connection. With multiple locations and a commitment to continuous growth, they've cultivated a brand rooted in intentional service, elevated aesthetics, and deep care for their clients and team. Grounded in a culture of excellence and evolution, the company is led by a visionary founder who blends business with purpose, creating a salon experience that is as soul-filled as it is stylish. Their dedication to excellence has earned national recognition, including features in major media outlets and accolades from platforms like CBS and The Knot.




What You'll Do

This is not a traditional executive assistant role. The client is seeking a proactive, senior-level professional who can serve as the founder's right hand, taking ownership of priorities, working independently, asking the right clarifying questions, and following through with minimal direction. Rather than simply executing assigned tasks, you will be expected to identify what needs to be done, prioritize effectively, push back when necessary, and help keep the founder accountable to key commitments. This is a long-term, full-time role best suited for someone who is highly organized, resourceful, and comfortable operating with a high degree of autonomy. Your responsibilities will include:




Executive Support & Founder Accountability: 40% Role Allocation

Own the founder's inbox: triage, draft responses, surface what needs attention, and clear what doesn't.
Own the founder's calendar: schedule, reschedule, defend protected time, and prepare daily briefings.
Take detailed notes in meetings and convert them into tracked action items.
Hold the founder accountable to what she commits to in meetings, proactively remind her of deadlines, deliverables, and outstanding promises.
Anticipate needs and surface issues before they become problems.




Operations & Vendor Management: 30% Role Allocation

Be the primary point of contact with vendors and service providers (electricity, internet, catering, accounting, software providers, and others).
Pull basic reports from Zenoti and other systems as requested; problem-solve to figure out how to get the data needed.
Send communications to clients through Zenoti CRM and other tools.
Organize scattered or undocumented SOPs into clean, actionable workflows.
Provide timely reminders to the founder for bills, payroll, accounting deliverables, and recurring obligations.




Client & Team Communication: 20% Role Allocation

Draft and send communications to clients and employees as directed by the founder.
Follow up on outstanding action items from team meetings until they are resolved.
Escalate issues that need the founder's attention with proposed solutions, not just problems.
Blended Personal Support: 10% Role Allocation
Handle personal tasks that intersect with work, exercised with strong discretion and confidentiality.




What You Should Have

Must-haves:

5+ years as an Executive Assistant, Chief of Staff, or senior administrative professional supporting a founder, CEO, or senior executive.
1+ year of remote work experience supporting clients in North America, Europe, Australia, or the UAE.
Experience supporting a Founder or Executive in a Beauty, Wellness, or Lifestyle brand.
Strong written and spoken English that is clear, professional, and confident in vendor and client communication.
Native-level fluency is not required; capability and experience are the priority.
Proven track record of working autonomously under ambiguity, taking scattered direction, and producing organized results.
Demonstrated ability to manage vendor relationships and external communications independently.
Experience working in environments where the founder/executive has very limited time to direct day-to-day work.
Strong written communication, comfortable drafting on behalf of an executive.
High discretion and confidentiality in this role have access to financial, personal, and sensitive business information.




Tech Stack:

Google Workspace (Gmail, Calendar, Drive, Docs, Sheets) - Fluency Required.
Slack and/or Microsoft Teams - Fluency Required.
